title: Electrohydrodynamic lubrication theory
authors: Anirban Chatterjee, Yacine Amarouchene, Thomas Salez
abstract: The free motion of charged colloids within ionic solutions and in the vicinity of charged boundaries, is a phenomenon that occurs in various natural, biological and industrial settings. Here, we develop an electrohydrodynamic lubrication theoretical framework, in order to characterize such a motion in the case of an infinite rigid cylinder near a rigid wall. Combining hydrodynamic lubrication theory, Debye-H\''uckel electrostatics, and Nernst-Planck electrokinetics, we derive the three coupled equations of motion for the normal, longitudinal and rotational degrees of freedom of the cylinder, which are then investigated numerically and through asymptotic analysis. Our results reveal complex behaviours, beyond existing asymptotic electroviscous-lift expressions, and extend the classical Faxen-Brenner-like mobility matrix when surface charges and dissolved ions are incorporated.
summary: This paper establishes a theoretical framework for understanding how electric charges on particles and surfaces influence their motion in fluids. By combining fluid mechanics with electrokinetics, the authors show that moving ions create an extra 'lift' force that pushes a cylinder away from a wall. The study provides new mathematical tools to predict how particles will move, slide, or rotate in industrial and biological environments where electric fields and ions are present.
main_problem: Characterizing the electrohydrodynamic motion and the emergence of symmetry-breaking lift forces for a charged cylinder moving near a charged boundary in an electrolyte.
main_result: The development of an electrohydrodynamic lubrication framework that predicts a generalized electroviscous lift force and an extended mobility matrix incorporating surface charges and ions.
method: The authors use lubrication theory, Debye-Huckel electrostatics, and Nernst-Planck electrokinetics to derive coupled nonlinear ordinary differential equations, which are analyzed via numerical simulations and asymptotic analysis.
model_or_system: An infinite rigid cylinder of radius 'a' moving in a Newtonian electrolyte near a rigid flat wall, considering the coupling of hydrodynamic, electrostatic, and electrokinetic forces.
key_observables: Normal distance (gap height), longitudinal position, rotation, streaming potential, and electroviscous lift force.
important_parameters: Debye screening length (kappa inverse), Péclet number (Pe), Hartmann number (Ha), surface potentials (zeta potentials), and the lubrication parameter (epsilon).
main_assumptions: Small-gap lubrication approximation (epsilon << 1), linearized Poisson-Boltzmann/Debye-Huckel electrostatics, and an open-circuit condition (no net current).
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the cylinder, wall, and electrolyte; Figure 4 displays the time evolution of dimensionless distance, longitudinal velocity, and angular velocity showing damped oscillations toward equilibrium.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the physical problem, defines the mathematical framework (hydrodynamics, electrostatics, electrokinetics), derives the coupled equations of motion, presents numerical results for various motion regimes (sedimentation and sliding), and concludes with asymptotic analysis and validation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
